g.data-narration: A Binary Search Tree is a binary tree where all left subtree values are less than root, and all right subtree values are greater.
g.text: What is a Binary Search Tree?
A binary tree with a special ordering property:

g.data-narration: Here's an example BST. Notice 8 has left subtree (1,3,4,6,7) all less than 8, and right subtree (10,13,14) all greater than 8.
Search compares the key with the current node: go left if key is smaller, right if larger. Time complexity is O(h) where h is height.
g.text: struct Node* search(struct Node *root, int key) {'{'}

g.data-narration: Insert finds the correct leaf position by comparing values, then adds the new node. Duplicate values are typically rejected.
g.text: struct Node* insert(struct Node *root, int key) {'{'}
• New node goes to left if < root
• New node goes to right if > root

g.data-narration: Find Minimum goes left until no more left children. Find Maximum goes right until no more right children. Both are O(h).
Deletion Case 1: Leaf node - simply remove it by returning NULL. No restructuring needed.
g.text: Simply remove - no children to reconnect:

g.data-narration: Deletion Case 2: One child - replace the deleted node with its child. Child takes parent's position in the tree.
g.text: Replace node with its single child:

g.data-narration: Deletion Case 3: Two children - find inorder successor (min in right subtree), copy its value, then delete the successor.
g.text: Find inorder successor (minimum in right subtree), replace node value, delete successor:

g.data-narration: Inorder traversal of BST always gives sorted order! Left-Root-Right visits nodes in ascending order.
g.text: • Validating if a tree is BST

g.data-narration: To validate BST, use min/max bounds. Each node must be within (min, max) range, updated recursively for subtrees.
g.text: int isBSTUtil(struct Node *root, int min, int max) {'{'}

g.data-narration: Time complexity depends on tree height. Balanced trees give O(log n), but skewed (degenerate) trees degrade to O(n).
BSTs are used in databases for indexing, compilers for symbol tables, dictionaries, and implementing priority queues.
g.text: Foundation for: AVL Trees, Red-Black Trees, B-Trees

g.data-narration: Remember: BST property is left less than root, right greater. Inorder gives sorted order. Height matters - balanced is O(log n), skewed is O(n).
g.text: Delete has 3 cases • Find Min (leftmost) • Find Max (rightmost)
